feed.title: Re: March 23rd 2021 Taproot Activation Meeting Notes

entry.title: Re: March 23rd 2021 Taproot Activation Meeting Notes
entry.summary:
The meeting held on April 6, 2021, centered on several critical aspects concerning the implementation and activation of certain technical proposals. A significant point of discussion was the adoption of a "speedy trial" variant to advance proposals without delay. The group noted unanimous agreement on the concept of a speedy trial, with specific interest in whether objections previously raised were still valid or had been addressed satisfactorily. The consensus leaned towards moving forward without waiting for any pending responses, indicating a readiness to progress irrespective of individual hesitations.
Another focal point was the choice between using heights and Median Time Past (MTP) as mechanisms for implementing changes. The decision between these two did not reach a robust consensus, highlighting divided opinions within the group. Technical safety concerns regarding both MTP and height were deemed unfounded, but questions about the necessity of additional reviews for height-based activation's safety remained unanswered.
Regarding parameter selection for releases, there was broad agreement on aiming for a release around May 1st, with specific timelines proposed for start and stop dates based on either heights or MTP. The group suggested an activation time around November 15th, emphasizing the significance of maintaining this date to avoid delays into less preferable times of the year. Flexibility around the schedule was discussed, with a general agreement on the importance of sticking to the November deadline to prevent pushing the release into a much later date.
The possibility of a Simultaneous User Activated Soft Fork (UASF) was also considered, with differing opinions on its feasibility and timing relative to the Speedy Trial (ST) client release. Luke-jr, in particular, expressed that a UASF client should be released before the ST client to facilitate adoption, a sentiment not widely shared among attendees. Compatibility between UASF and MTP-based ST by selecting matching heights for start and stop was acknowledged, although concerns were raised about how the choice between MTP and height could impact the potential for contentious forks and the timing of UASF releases.
This summary encapsulates the key discussions and viewpoints presented during the meeting, reflecting a collective effort to navigate complex technical decisions and achieve consensus on the way forward for implementing and activating new proposals.
